# Language Coverage Checklist: SPEC-023 - OCaml Language Support

**Purpose**: Validate that SPEC-023 language-coverage requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable before implementation tasks are generated.
**Created**: 2026-07-05
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are `.ml` and `.mli` recognition requirements complete for implementation files, interface files, unpaired source files, and unpaired interface files? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §Edge Cases, Contract §File Recognition and Status]
- [x] CHK002 Are broad OCaml constructs from the design concept fully represented, including modules, signatures, functors, types, records, variants, values, functions, let-bindings, classes, objects, labeled and optional arguments, local modules, first-class modules, GADTs, polymorphic variants, attributes, extension nodes, and pattern-heavy definitions? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, Plan §Technical Context, Quickstart §3]
- [x] CHK003 Are interface declaration requirements complete for value, type, record, variant/GADT/polymorphic-variant, module, module type/signature/class type, class, method, `open`, and `include` declarations? [Completeness, Spec §Clarifications, Research §Interface declarations emit public symbol kinds]
- [x] CHK004 Are source-level node-kind and source-span requirements specified precisely for functors, named function-like lets, pattern-only lets, classes/objects, labeled/optional parameters, anonymous nested definitions, and unsupported advanced syntax visibility? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-004, Contract §Symbol Output]
- [x] CHK005 Are `.ml`/`.mli` pairing requirements complete for unique same-directory pairs, source-only files, interface-only files, and ambiguous pairings? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007, Data Model §OCaml Interface Pairing]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is "useful source spans" clarified with objective expectations for declaration starts, body coverage, and nearest useful owner behavior? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK007 Are labeled and optional argument requirements specific enough to distinguish stable parameter/name evidence from unstable pattern text? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005, Contract §Symbol Output]
- [x] CHK008 Are attributes and extension nodes clearly bounded as parse-preserved syntax without PPX expansion or speculative generated symbols? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-014, Contract §Fail-Closed Behavior]
- [x] CHK009 Are advanced-syntax limitations worded so unsupported constructs remain visible enough without implying precise graph behavior that is out of scope? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §PPX Policy]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK010 Do spec, plan, research, data model, contract, and quickstart agree that both `tree-sitter-ocaml.wasm` and `tree-sitter-ocaml_interface.wasm` are required? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Plan §Technical Context, Research §Vendor tree-sitter-ocaml, Contract §File Recognition and Status]
- [x] CHK011 Do all artifacts agree that source/interface pairing is same-directory, same-basename, and unique-only? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Research §.ml and .mli pairing, Data Model §OCaml Interface Pairing, Contract §Relationship Output]
- [x] CHK012 Do node-kind requirements use only existing CodeGraph node kinds and avoid introducing OCaml-specific graph vocabulary? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, Contract §Symbol Output]
- [x] CHK013 Do syntax-coverage requirements stay aligned with the fail-closed rule for ambiguous module, package, and PPX cases? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, Contract §Fail-Closed Behavior]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK014 Can SC-001 be objectively measured against 100% of the required construct inventory, including node kind, span, and containment expectations? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001, Quickstart §3]
- [x] CHK015 Are the real-repository smoke and nine retrieval probes tied back to OCaml language coverage rather than only general validation evidence? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-002, Spec §SC-003, Quickstart §4-5]
- [x] CHK016 Are graph-stability and no-speculative-edge success criteria measurable for advanced syntax that can otherwise silently drop or invent symbols? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-004, Spec §SC-005, Data Model §Validation Corpus]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK017 Are nested modules, functors, local opens, includes, shadowed names, source-only files, interface-only files, and multi-package workspace boundaries addressed as requirements instead of deferred implementation guesses? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-008]
- [x] CHK018 Are pattern-heavy definitions, anonymous nested definitions, labeled arguments, optional arguments, attributes, and extension nodes covered as explicit edge cases that may otherwise silently drop useful symbols? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-005, Research §First-slice syntax is broad but static]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK019 Are grammar-source, parser-health, and copied-artifact requirements traceable to the selected implementation and interface grammars? [Dependency, Spec §Clarifications, Research §Health-check parser artifacts, Data Model §OCaml Grammar Artifact]
- [x] CHK020 Are OCaml LSP precision, PPX expansion, typechecker-grade semantics, package nodes, and external package edges consistently excluded while still defining source-level fallback behavior? [Scope, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Summary, Contract §Non-Contract]
